senses.definition: a type of wood used for making skewers in cooking, often from hardwoods that can withstand heat
examples: We grilled vegetables on bamboo skewers, but for a rustic touch we used skewerwood.
The recipe calls for skewers made of strong skewerwood to avoid burning.

senses.definition: the most skewed or tilted; most biased or distorted
examples: Of all the opinions, that one is the skewest.
The report presented the skewest data to support their claim.

senses.definition: the act or process of tilting, bending, or distorting something away from a vertical or true line
examples: The skewing of the propeller changed the flight dynamics.
Common distortions in measurement come from data skewing.

senses.definition: plural of skewing; multiple instances of tilting or distortion
examples: The report noted several skewings in the data.
There were skewings in the sample distributions.

senses.definition: slang spelling of 'school' referring to educational institution; often used in internet slang
examples: He skipped skewl to hang out with friends.
The meme shows a classroom scene from skewl.

senses.definition: in a slanted or biased manner; not straight or even
examples: The camera was skewly angled to hide the corner.
A skewly drawn chart can mislead viewers.

senses.definition: a measure of how asymmetrical a distribution is
examples: The skewness of the data indicated a long tail on one side.
Skewness helps describe the shape of a distribution.

senses.definition: plural of skewness; multiple measures of asymmetry in distributions
examples: Several skewnesses were reported across the datasets.
Researchers compared the skewnesses to identify anomalies.

senses.definition: to bias or distort information, data, or perceptions; to tilt or slant
examples: The report skews the results to favor the sponsor.
Beware how sampling can skew conclusions.

senses.definition: slightly off, not quite straight or aligned; somewhat odd
examples: The painting hung skewwhiff, giving the room a quirky charm.
Her hat sat skewwhiff after the wind.

senses.definition: in a skewwise direction; tilted or slanted
examples: The rope hung skewwise from the beam.
The picture leaned skewwise on the wall.

senses.definition: slightly crooked or biased; not straight or honest
examples: The report might be skewy in favor of the client.
That testimony sounded skewy to me.

senses.definition: a misspelling or informal variant of 'ski' or 'key', depending on context; may appear in dialectal usage or as a placeholder
examples: Push the sKEY into the lock.

senses.definition: rare or obsolete form related to 'sketching' or 'skeyting' as a dialectal spelling; not common in modern usage
examples: Old manuscripts mention skeyting in marginal notes.

senses.definition: unknown or improper coinage; no established sense in contemporary English
examples: Skhian is not a recognized English word in standard dictionaries.

senses.definition: to travel on skis, gliding on snow
examples: We will ski down the mountain tomorrow.
She learned to ski as a child.

senses.definition: a long narrow piece of wood or metal used for support or a sport; equipment for skiing
examples: The ski was damaged after the fall.
He bought new skis for the season.

senses.definition: capable of being skied; suitable for skiing
examples: The mountain is skiable after fresh snowfall.
Not all slopes are skiable in the morning light.

senses.definition: an X-ray image of a skiing-related structure or an obsolete term for radiographic image; extremely rare in modern usage
examples: In older medical texts, skiagrams referred to X-ray images.

senses.definition: plural of skiagram; obsolete or rare term for radiographic images
examples: Doctors discussed skiagrams from the old hospital archive.

senses.definition: a radiographic image; an old printing term for an image photographically reproduced
examples: The ancient text included a skiagraph of a temple.

senses.definition: to have created or produced a skiagraph; to have captured an X-ray image historically
examples: The bones were skiagraphed to study the fracture.

senses.definition: a person who creates skiagrams; an old term for radiograph technician
examples: The skiagrapher inspected the image for clarity.

senses.definition: relating to skiographs or radiographic images
examples: A skiagraphic study showed the anomaly clearly.

senses.definition: relating to skiographs; of or resembling radiographic images
examples: Skiagraphical data were referenced in the report.

senses.definition: in a skiagraphical (radiographic) manner
examples: The diagram was skiagraphically rendered for clarity.
